metadata.content:
# LED Strip Animation Controller with Pattern Configuration

## Overview
This template implements an advanced LED strip animation controller with multiple built-in patterns and web-based control interface. Perfect for decorative lighting, ambient effects, and visual displays.

## Features
- Multiple animation patterns (Rainbow, Chase, Fade, Sparkle, etc.)
- Web-based control interface (ESP32)
- Real-time pattern switching
- Adjustable speed and brightness
- Configurable LED count and color order
- JSON API for integration
- Mobile-responsive web interface
- Pattern customization support

## Built-in Patterns
- **Rainbow**: Smooth rainbow color cycling
- **Chase**: LED chase effect with selectable color
- **Fade**: Fade in/out effect with breathing animation
- **Sparkle**: Random sparkle effect
- **Color Wipe**: Color wipe across the strip
- **Theater Chase**: Theater chase effect
- **Scanner**: Scanner/Knight rider effect
- **Solid**: Solid color fill

## Web Interface Features
- **Pattern Selection**: Quick access to all patterns
- **Speed Control**: Adjustable animation speed (1-10)
- **Brightness Control**: Global brightness adjustment (0-255)
- **Real-time Status**: Current pattern and settings display
- **Pattern Buttons**: Visual pattern selection
- **Control Sliders**: Intuitive speed and brightness control
- **Action Buttons**: Pause, clear, and random pattern selection

## Configuration Options
- **LED Pin**: Data pin for LED strip (default: 6)
- **LED Count**: Number of LEDs (1-300, default: 60)
- **Brightness**: Initial brightness (0-255, default: 128)
- **Color Order**: LED strip color order (RGB/GRB/BRG, default: GRB)
- **Animation Speed**: Default speed multiplier (1-10, default: 5)
- **Web Control**: Enable/disable web interface
- **Custom Patterns**: User-defined patterns

## Power Requirements
- **Voltage**: 5V DC for most LED strips
- **Current**: ~60mA per meter for WS2812B LEDs
- **Power Calculation**: LED Count × 60mA × Voltage
- **Capacitor**: 1000µF capacitor recommended for power injection

## Wiring
- LED Strip 5V → Arduino 5V
- LED Strip GND → Arduino GND
- LED Strip Data In → Arduino Digital Pin {{.ledPin}}
- Capacitor (1000µF) → Parallel to LED Strip power connections

**Power Injection for Long Strips**:
- Every 1-2 meters, inject power from separate 5V supply
- Use appropriate gauge wire for current capacity
- Add capacitors at injection points

## API Endpoints (ESP32)
- `GET /` - Main web interface
- `POST /control` - Control pattern, speed, and brightness
- `GET /status` - Get current status
- `GET /patterns` - Get available patterns

## Control API
Send POST request to `/control`:
```
pattern=rainbow&speed=5&brightness=128
```

## Response Format
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "pattern": "rainbow",
  "speed": 5,
  "patterns": 5,
  "brightness": 128,
  "numLeds": 60,
  "colorOrder": "GRB"
}
```

## Pattern Customization
Each pattern can be customized:
- **Color Selection**: Choose specific colors for patterns
- **Speed Adjustment**: Individual speed control per pattern
- **Brightness**: Global brightness affects all patterns
- **LED Count**: Automatically adjusts to strip length

## Use Cases
- **Home Lighting**: Ambient lighting effects
- **Event Lighting**: Party and event decorations
- **Architectural Lighting**: Building and space illumination
- **Signage**: Dynamic sign and display lighting
- **Art Installations**: Creative lighting projects
- **Stage Lighting**: Performance and theater lighting
- **Holiday Decorations**: Seasonal lighting effects

## Integration Options
- **Sensor Integration**: Motion-activated patterns
- **Time Scheduling**: Automated pattern changes
- **Music Synchronization**: Audio-reactive lighting
- **Voice Control**: Integration with voice assistants
- **Mobile Apps**: Remote control applications
- **IoT Platforms**: Integration with smart home systems

## Dependencies
- FastLED library v3.5.0
- ArduinoJson v6.21.3
- WiFi library (ESP32 built-in)
- WebServer library (ESP32 built-in)

## Troubleshooting
- **LED Strip Not Working**: Check power supply and data connections
- **Flickering Issues**: Add capacitor near power injection points
- **Color Issues**: Verify color order configuration
- **Web Interface Not Loading**: Check WiFi connection (ESP32)
- **Slow Performance**: Reduce LED count or optimize code
- **Power Issues**: Use adequate power supply and proper wire gauge

## Advanced Features
- **Multi-Strip Control**: Control multiple strips independently
- **Synchronization**: Coordinate multiple controllers
- **Real-time Data**: Display sensor data on LEDs
- **Image Display**: Convert images to LED patterns
- **Sound Reactive**: Audio visualization effects
- **Network Sync**: Synchronize patterns across devices

## Performance Specifications
- **Update Rate**: 20-100Hz depending on pattern complexity
- **Power Consumption**: 3.6W per meter at full brightness
- **Response Time**: <100ms for pattern changes
- **Memory Usage**: ~3KB for LED buffer + program
- **Processor Load**: 10-30% depending on pattern complexity

## Safety Considerations
- **Power Supply**: Use appropriate power supply for LED count
- **Heat Management**: Monitor for overheating with long strips
- **Current Limits**: Don't exceed 3A per data line
- **Voltage Regulation**: Use stable 5V power supply
- **EMI Considerations**: Add filtering for sensitive applications

## Maintenance
- **Regular Inspection**: Check connections and power supply
- **Cleaning**: Keep LED strip clean for optimal brightness
- **Software Updates**: Update patterns and features
- **Performance Monitoring**: Monitor for performance issues
- **Backup Settings**: Save custom configurations
